children.text: contains a photograph of the individual and such other personal identifying information relating to the individual as the Attorney General finds, by regulation, sufficient for purposes of this subsection,

children.label: II
children.text: is evidence of authorization of employment in the United States, and

children.label: III
children.text: contains security features to make it resistant to tampering, counterfeiting, and fraudulent use.

children.label: i
children.text: social security account number card (other than such a card which specifies on the face that the issuance of the card does not authorize employment in the United States); or

children.label: ii
children.text: other documentation evidencing authorization of employment in the United States which the Attorney General finds, by regulation, to be acceptable for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Documents establishing identity of individual
children.label: D Documents establishing identity of individual
i
children.text: driver’s license or similar document issued for the purpose of identification by a State, if it contains a photograph of the individual or such other personal identifying information relating to the individual as the Attorney General finds, by regulation, sufficient for purposes of this section; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of individuals under 16 years of age or in a State which does not provide for issuance of an identification document (other than a driver’s license) referred to in clause (i), documentation of personal identity of such other type as the Attorney General finds, by regulation, provides a reliable means of identification.

children.heading: Authority to prohibit use of certain documents
children.label: E Authority to prohibit use of certain documents
children.text: If the Attorney General finds, by regulation, that any document described in subparagraph (B), (C), or (D) as establishing employment authorization or identity does not reliably establish such authorization or identity or is being used fraudulently to an unacceptable degree, the Attorney General may prohibit or place conditions on its use for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Individual attestation of employment authorization
children.label: 2 Individual attestation of employment authorization
children.text: The individual must attest, under penalty of perjury on the form designated or established for purposes of paragraph (1), that the individual is a citizen or national of the United States, an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence, or an alien who is authorized under this chapter or by the Attorney General to be hired, recruited, or referred for such employment. Such attestation may be manifested by either a hand-written or an electronic signature.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of the recruiting or referral for a fee (without hiring) of an individual, three years after the date of the recruiting or referral, and

children.label: i
children.text: three years after the date of such hiring, or

children.label: ii
children.text: one year after the date the individual’s employment is terminated,

children.heading: Copying of documentation permitted
children.label: 4 Copying of documentation permitted
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the person or entity may copy a document presented by an individual pursuant to this subsection and may retain the copy, but only (except as otherwise permitted under law) for the purpose of complying with the requirements of this subsection.

children.heading: Limitation on use of attestation form
children.label: 5 Limitation on use of attestation form
children.text: A form designated or established by the Attorney General under this subsection and any information contained in or appended to such form, may not be used for purposes other than for enforcement of this chapter and sections 1001, 1028, 1546, and 1621 of title 18.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraphs (B) and (C), a person or entity is considered to have complied with a requirement of this subsection notwithstanding a technical or procedural failure to meet such requirement if there was a good faith attempt to comply with the requirement.

children.heading: Exception if failure to correct after notice
children.label: B Exception if failure to correct after notice
i
children.text: the Service (or another enforcement agency) has explained to the person or entity the basis for the failure,

children.label: ii
children.text: the person or entity has been provided a period of not less than 10 business days (beginning after the date of the explanation) within which to correct the failure, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the person or entity has not corrected the failure voluntarily within such period.

children.heading: Exception for pattern or practice violators
children.label: C Exception for pattern or practice violators
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to a person or entity that has or is engaging in a pattern or practice of violations of subsection (a)(1)(A) or (a)(2).

children.heading: No authorization of national identification cards
children.label: c No authorization of national identification cards
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to authorize, directly or indirectly, the issuance or use of national identification cards or the establishment of a national identification card.

children.heading: Evaluation and changes in employment verification system
children.label: d Evaluation and changes in employment verification system

children.heading: Presidential monitoring and improvements in system
children.label: 1 Presidential monitoring and improvements in system

children.heading: Monitoring
children.label: A Monitoring
children.text: The President shall provide for the monitoring and evaluation of the degree to which the employment verification system established under subsection (b) provides a secure system to determine employment eligibility in the United States and shall examine the suitability of existing Federal and State identification systems for use for this purpose.

children.heading: Improvements to establish secure system
children.label: B Improvements to establish secure system
children.text: To the extent that the system established under subsection (b) is found not to be a secure system to determine employment eligibility in the United States, the President shall, subject to paragraph (3) and taking into account the results of any demonstration projects conducted under paragraph (4), implement such changes in (including additions to) the requirements of subsection (b) as may be necessary to establish a secure system to determine employment eligibility in the United States. Such changes in the system may be implemented only if the changes conform to the requirements of paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: a person with the identity claimed by an employee or prospective employee is eligible to work, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the employee or prospective employee is claiming the identity of another individual.

children.heading: Using of counterfeit-resistant documents
children.label: B Using of counterfeit-resistant documents
children.text: If the system requires that a document be presented to or examined by an employer, the document must be in a form which is resistant to counterfeiting and tampering.

children.heading: Limited use of system
children.label: C Limited use of system
children.text: Any personal information utilized by the system may not be made available to Government agencies, employers, and other persons except to the extent necessary to verify that an individual is not an unauthorized alien.

children.heading: Privacy of information
children.label: D Privacy of information
children.text: The system must protect the privacy and security of personal information and identifiers utilized in the system.

children.heading: Limited denial of verification
children.label: E Limited denial of verification
children.text: A verification that an employee or prospective employee is eligible to be employed in the United States may not be withheld or revoked under the system for any reason other than that the employee or prospective employee is an unauthorized alien.

children.heading: Limited use for law enforcement purposes
children.label: F Limited use for law enforcement purposes
children.text: The system may not be used for law enforcement purposes, other than for enforcement of this chapter or sections 1001, 1028, 1546, and 1621 of title 18.

children.heading: Restriction on use of new documents
children.label: G Restriction on use of new documents
children.text: If the system requires individuals to present a new card or other document (designed specifically for use for this purpose) at the time of hiring, recruitment, or referral, then such document may not be required to be presented for any purpose other than under this chapter (or enforcement of sections 1001, 1028, 1546, and 1621 of title 18) nor to be carried on one’s person.

children.heading: Notice to Congress before implementing changes
children.label: 3 Notice to Congress before implementing changes
i
children.text: 60 days,

children.label: ii
children.text: one year, in the case of a major change described in subparagraph (D)(iii), or

children.label: iii
children.text: two years, in the case of a major change described in clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (D),

children.heading: Contents of report
children.label: B Contents of report
children.text: In any report under subparagraph (A) the President shall include recommendations for the establishment of civil and criminal sanctions for unauthorized use or disclosure of the information or identifiers contained in such system.

children.heading: Congressional review of major changes
children.label: C Congressional review of major changes

children.heading: Hearings and review
children.label: i Hearings and review
children.text: The Committees on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives and of the Senate shall cause to have printed in the Congressional Record the substance of any major change described in subparagraph (D), shall hold hearings respecting the feasibility and desirability of implementing such a change, and, within the two year period before implementation, shall report to their respective Houses findings on whether or not such a change should be implemented.

children.heading: Congressional action
children.label: ii Congressional action
children.text: No major change may be implemented unless the Congress specifically provides, in an appropriations or other Act, for funds for implementation of the change.

children.label: i
children.text: require an individual to present a new card or other document (designed specifically for use for this purpose) at the time of hiring, recruitment, or referral,

children.label: ii
children.text: provide for a telephone verification system under which an employer, recruiter, or referrer must transmit to a Federal official information concerning the immigration status of prospective employees and the official transmits to the person, and the person must record, a verification code, or

children.label: iii
children.text: require any change in any card used for accounting purposes under the Social Security Act [ et seq.], including any change requiring that the only social security account number cards which may be presented in order to comply with subsection (b)(1)(C)(i) are such cards as are in a counterfeit-resistant form consistent with the second sentence of section 205(c)(2)(D) of the Social Security Act [].42 U.S.C. 30142 U.S.C. 405(c)(2)(D)

children.heading: General revenue funding of social security card changes
children.label: E General revenue funding of social security card changes
children.text: Any costs incurred in developing and implementing any change described in subparagraph (D)(iii) for purposes of this subsection shall not be paid for out of any trust fund established under the Social Security Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 301

children.heading: Authority
children.label: A Authority
children.text: The President may undertake demonstration projects (consistent with paragraph (2)) of different changes in the requirements of subsection (b). No such project may extend over a period of longer than five years.

children.heading: Reports on projects
children.label: B Reports on projects
children.text: The President shall report to the Congress on the results of demonstration projects conducted under this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: for individuals and entities to file written, signed complaints respecting potential violations of subsection (a) or (g)(1),

children.label: B
children.text: for the investigation of those complaints which, on their face, have a substantial probability of validity,

children.label: C
children.text: for the investigation of such other violations of subsection (a) or (g)(1) as the Attorney General determines to be appropriate, and

children.label: D
children.text: for the designation in the Service of a unit which has, as its primary duty, the prosecution of cases of violations of subsection (a) or (g)(1) under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: immigration officers and administrative law judges shall have reasonable access to examine evidence of any person or entity being investigated,

children.label: B
children.text: administrative law judges, may, if necessary, compel by subpoena the attendance of witnesses and the production of evidence at any designated place or hearing, and

children.label: C
children.text: immigration officers designated by the Commissioner may compel by subpoena the attendance of witnesses and the production of evidence at any designated place prior to the filing of a complaint in a case under paragraph (2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Before imposing an order described in paragraph (4), (5), or (6) against a person or entity under this subsection for a violation of subsection (a) or (g)(1), the Attorney General shall provide the person or entity with notice and, upon request made within a reasonable time (of not less than 30 days, as established by the Attorney General) of the date of the notice, a hearing respecting the violation.

children.heading: Conduct of hearing
children.label: B Conduct of hearing
children.text: Any hearing so requested shall be conducted before an administrative law judge. The hearing shall be conducted in accordance with the requirements of . The hearing shall be held at the nearest practicable place to the place where the person or entity resides or of the place where the alleged violation occurred. If no hearing is so requested, the Attorney General’s imposition of the order shall constitute a final and unappealable order.section 554 of title 5

children.heading: Issuance of orders
children.label: C Issuance of orders
children.text: If the administrative law judge determines, upon the preponderance of the evidence received, that a person or entity named in the complaint has violated subsection (a) or (g)(1), the administrative law judge shall state his findings of fact and issue and cause to be served on such person or entity an order described in paragraph (4), (5), or (6).

children.heading: Cease and desist order with civil money penalty for hiring, recruiting, and referral violations
children.label: 4 Cease and desist order with civil money penalty for hiring, recruiting, and referral violations
i
children.text: not less than $250 and not more than $2,000 for each unauthorized alien with respect to whom a violation of either such subsection occurred,

children.label: ii
children.text: not less than $2,000 and not more than $5,000 for each such alien in the case of a person or entity previously subject to one order under this paragraph, or

children.label: iii
children.text: not less than $3,000 and not more than $10,000 for each such alien in the case of a person or entity previously subject to more than one order under this paragraph; and

children.label: i
children.text: to comply with the requirements of subsection (b) (or subsection (d) if applicable) with respect to individuals hired (or recruited or referred for employment for a fee) during a period of up to three years, and

children.label: ii
children.text: to take such other remedial action as is appropriate.

children.heading: Order for civil money penalty for paperwork violations
children.label: 5 Order for civil money penalty for paperwork violations
children.text: With respect to a violation of subsection (a)(1)(B), the order under this subsection shall require the person or entity to pay a civil penalty in an amount of not less than $100 and not more than $1,000 for each individual with respect to whom such violation occurred. In determining the amount of the penalty, due consideration shall be given to the size of the business of the employer being charged, the good faith of the employer, the seriousness of the violation, whether or not the individual was an unauthorized alien, and the history of previous violations.

children.heading: Order for prohibited indemnity bonds
children.label: 6 Order for prohibited indemnity bonds
children.text: With respect to a violation of subsection (g)(1), the order under this subsection may provide for the remedy described in subsection (g)(2).

children.heading: Administrative appellate review
children.label: 7 Administrative appellate review
children.text: The decision and order of an administrative law judge shall become the final agency decision and order of the Attorney General unless either (A) within 30 days, an official delegated by regulation to exercise review authority over the decision and order modifies or vacates the decision and order, or (B) within 30 days of the date of such a modification or vacation (or within 60 days of the date of decision and order of an administrative law judge if not so modified or vacated) the decision and order is referred to the Attorney General pursuant to regulations, in which case the decision and order of the Attorney General shall become the final agency decision and order under this subsection. The Attorney General may not delegate the Attorney General’s authority under this paragraph to any entity which has review authority over immigration-related matters.

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: 8 Judicial review
children.text: A person or entity adversely affected by a final order respecting an assessment may, within 45 days after the date the final order is issued, file a petition in the Court of Appeals for the appropriate circuit for review of the order.

children.heading: Enforcement of orders
children.label: 9 Enforcement of orders
children.text: If a person or entity fails to comply with a final order issued under this subsection against the person or entity, the Attorney General shall file a suit to seek compliance with the order in any appropriate district court of the United States. In any such suit, the validity and appropriateness of the final order shall not be subject to review.

children.heading: Criminal penalties and injunctions for pattern or practice violations
children.label: f Criminal penalties and injunctions for pattern or practice violations

children.heading: Criminal penalty
children.label: 1 Criminal penalty
children.text: Any person or entity which engages in a pattern or practice of violations of subsection (a)(1)(A) or (a)(2) shall be fined not more than $3,000 for each unauthorized alien with respect to whom such a violation occurs, imprisoned for not more than six months for the entire pattern or practice, or both, notwithstanding the provisions of any other Federal law relating to fine levels.

children.heading: Enjoining of pattern or practice violations
children.label: 2 Enjoining of pattern or practice violations
children.text: Whenever the Attorney General has reasonable cause to believe that a person or entity is engaged in a pattern or practice of employment, recruitment, or referral in violation of paragraph (1)(A) or (2) of subsection (a), the Attorney General may bring a civil action in the appropriate district court of the United States requesting such relief, including a permanent or temporary injunction, restraining order, or other order against the person or entity, as the Attorney General deems necessary.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: 1 Prohibition
children.text: It is unlawful for a person or other entity, in the hiring, recruiting, or referring for employment of any individual, to require the individual to post a bond or security, to pay or agree to pay an amount, or otherwise to provide a financial guarantee or indemnity, against any potential liability arising under this section relating to such hiring, recruiting, or referring of the individual.

children.heading: Civil penalty
children.label: 2 Civil penalty
children.text: Any person or entity which is determined, after notice and opportunity for an administrative hearing under subsection (e), to have violated paragraph (1) shall be subject to a civil penalty of $1,000 for each violation and to an administrative order requiring the return of any amounts received in violation of such paragraph to the employee or, if the employee cannot be located, to the general fund of the Treasury.

children.heading: Documentation
children.label: 1 Documentation
children.text: In providing documentation or endorsement of authorization of aliens (other than aliens lawfully admitted for permanent residence) authorized to be employed in the United States, the Attorney General shall provide that any limitations with respect to the period or type of employment or employer shall be conspicuously stated on the documentation or endorsement.

children.heading: Preemption
children.label: 2 Preemption
children.text: The provisions of this section preempt any State or local law imposing civil or criminal sanctions (other than through licensing and similar laws) upon those who employ, or recruit or refer for a fee for employment, unauthorized aliens.

children.heading: Definition of unauthorized alien
children.label: 3 Definition of unauthorized alien
children.text: As used in this section, the term “unauthorized alien” means, with respect to the employment of an alien at a particular time, that the alien is not at that time either (A) an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence, or (B) authorized to be so employed by this chapter or by the Attorney General.
